title: Commissioner Lahbib in Lebanon to reiterate EU's strong humanitarian commitment and welcome EU's 7th humanitarian flight
content: This Humanitarian Air Bridge will deliver essential health and shelter items – from the EU's own stock and its partners – to the most vulnerable, reaffirming the European Union's solidarity with Lebanon and steadfast humanitarian support to the country.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: Commissioner Lahbib visits Cyprus to discuss fire preparedness and inclusive Europe
content: The discussions take place after the adoption of the Commission Communication on integrated wildfire risk management and few days after the Commission adopted its Proposal for a Council Recommendation on this issue.
snippet: The discussions take place after the adoption of the Commission Communication on integrated wildfire risk management and few days after the Commission adopted its Proposal for a Council Recommendation
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: Acute food insecurity and malnutrition remain alarmingly high as crises deepen
content: Acute food insecurity and malnutrition levels remain alarmingly high and deeply entrenched, with crises increasingly concentrated in a core group of countries, according to the Global Report on Food Crises 2026, released today by an international alliance.

title: EU announces €235 million in humanitarian aid for West and Central Africa
content: The European Commission announced today €235 million in humanitarian assistance to support the most vulnerable in West and Central Africa. This funding will support those affected by current conflicts and food crises, forcibly displaced people, host communities, and hard-to-reach populations.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: 5,000 patients medically evacuated from Ukraine for urgent treatment
content: Since the start of Russia’s war of aggression, the EU has worked around-the-clock with Member States and partners to ensure that Ukrainian patients in urgent need of treatment can access care beyond the country’s borders.

title: EU and Member States pledge over €812 million in response to crisis in Sudan
content: EU aid will address food insecurity through cash assistance, healthcare and nutrition services, water and sanitation, shelter support, protection and education. This funding will also help sustain essential services, support livelihoods, and strengthen resilience.

title: EU releases additional €2 million in humanitarian aid for the Cuban population
content: This funding will contribute to provide logistics support to humanitarian partners delivering urgent relief to the most vulnerable, due to the current energy crisis and related difficulties on the distribution of food and drinking water.

title: Record number of wildfires in 2025 calls for stronger EU action
content: The European Union experienced its most devastating wildfire season on record in 2025, with over 1 million hectares of land burnt – an area roughly the size of Cyprus – according to new data from the European Forest Fire Information System.

title: EU steps up support for disaster preparedness and humanitarian aid in Asia
content: The European Commission has released nearly €11 million to support humanitarian aid and strengthen disaster preparedness across Asia in 2026.
snippet: The European Commission has released nearly €11 million to support humanitarian aid and strengthen disaster preparedness across Asia in 2026.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commission scales up its humanitarian response in Lebanon to address urgent needs
content: The Commission has scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of live-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country.
snippet: The Commission has scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of live-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: Commission launches new strategy to tackle rising wildfire threat
content: Wildfires pose a growing threat to Europe, with climate change increasing their intensity and frequency. In line with the Preparedness Union Strategy, this communication provides guidance on how to better prevent, prepare for, respond and recover from wildfires.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU crisis reserve helps in Cyprus’ foot and mouth disease response
content: The mobilisation follows a request for assistance from the Cypriot authorities via the EU Civil Protection Mechanism. It comprises disinfectants, personal protective equipment, disposable coveralls, face masks, shoe covers, and other items essential for curbing the further spread of the virus.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: EU hosts high-level conference to mark 1 year of its Preparedness Union Strategy
content: Launched in March 2025, the strategy responds to a changing world in which geopolitical shifts, climate events and technological risks increasingly call for a holistic and coordinated European approach; one that places preparedness at the heart of EU policy.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: Commission invites 150 EU citizens to help shape future preparedness actions
content: The 1st session of the European Citizens' Panel on Preparedness will start in Brussels and will run until Sunday. 150 randomly selected citizens from across Europe will discuss recommendations on how to implement the Preparedness Union Strategy.

title: Kabul rehabilitation centre strike: joint statement by High Representative/Vice President Kallas and Commissioner Lahbib
content: We call on both sides to exercise maximum restraint and take all feasible measures to minimise the risk of harming civilians.
snippet: We call on both sides to exercise maximum restraint and take all feasible measures to minimise the risk of harming civilians.

title: EU announces €36 million in humanitarian aid to Mozambique and neighbouring countries in Southern Africa
content: The European Commission announced €36 million in EU humanitarian aid that will reach 6 countries across Southern Africa and the Indian Ocean region. Millions of people face acute food insecurity, and conflict in northern Mozambique.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: EU announces €458 million in humanitarian aid for the war-torn Middle East
content: With major donors withdrawing from the region and international humanitarian law under unprecedented strain, the EU is sustaining life-saving assistance to millions of people.
snippet: With major donors withdrawing from the region and international humanitarian law under unprecedented strain, the EU is sustaining life-saving assistance to millions of people.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU-funded flight repatriates 134 more European citizens from the Middle East, bringing total repatriations to over 11,000 in recent weeks
content: The 5th evacuation flight landed in Bucharest, Romania. Fully funded by the EU, the flight enabled the safe repatriation of 134 European citizens, following a request from the Romanian authorities. The ERCC has supported near 90 flights, bringing over 11,000 European citizens home.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: EU brings over 8,000 European citizens home amid Middle East escalation
content: 2 repatriation flights chartered directly by the European Commission have just landed in Warsaw, Poland, bringing back 348 European citizens that were stranded in the Middle East.
snippet: 2 repatriation flights chartered directly by the European Commission have just landed in Warsaw, Poland, bringing back 348 European citizens that were stranded in the Middle East.
sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations
sourceDescription: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations | News

title: EU mobilises emergency humanitarian aid for Lebanon
content: The EU has mobilised emergency humanitarian aid consisting of food medical kits, shelter materials, recreation kits, and winter clothing kits, to meet the urgent, rising humanitarian needs in Lebanon, where over 680,000 people have been displaced in recent days due to ongoing hostilities.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: EU steps up civil protection efforts and brings back 356 citizens home amid developments in the Middle East
content: 2 repatriation flights chartered directly by the European Commission have safely landed in Romania. The 2 flights brought back 356 European citizens who were stranded in the Middle East from Oman to Romania.

title: Joint statement by President von der Leyen and President Costa after the video conference with Leaders from Middle East countries on the war in Iran
content: The Presidents expressed deep concern about the impact of the regional crisis on Lebanon. President von der Leyen announced a mobilisation of ReliefEU stocks to support some 130,000 persons in Lebanon with a first flight planned for tomorrow.
snippet: The Presidents expressed deep concern about the impact of the regional crisis on Lebanon. President von der Leyen announced a mobilisation of ReliefEU stocks to support some 130,000 persons in Lebanon

title: Commission coordinates repatriation flights from the Middle East under the EU Civil Protection Mechanism
content: Yesterday and today, the European Commission's Emergency Response Coordination Centre (ERCC) has supported EU Member States with the organisation of 6 repatriation flights, bringing European citizens safely back to Bulgaria, Italy, Austria, and Slovakia.

sourceTitle: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations

title: EU announces €63 million for Somalia to respond to the worsening humanitarian crisis
content: This announcement comes as drought related food insecurity and malnutrition rates are surging nationwide in Somalia, with 6.5 million people (i.e. around 1 in 3 Somalis) facing hunger and an estimated 1.85 million children under the age of 5 at risk of acute malnutrition.

title: Sudan: joint statement on protection of civilians and humanitarian operations
content: Following the recent severe escalation in the conflict in Sudan, Commissioner Hadja Lahbib joined 32 countries to reiterate EU's full support to the people of Sudan and reaffirm its commitment to the respect of international humanitarian law.

title: EU announces over €81 million in humanitarian aid as conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo pushes Great Lakes crisis to new extremes
content: With this new humanitarian funding, the EU will provide life-saving services to the most vulnerable in the eastern DRC, but also to Congolese refugees forced to flee the country.
snippet: With this new humanitarian funding, the EU will provide life-saving services to the most vulnerable in the eastern DRC, but also to Congolese refugees forced to flee the country.

title: Commissioner Lahbib visits Ukraine to reiterate EU support
content: While Russia continues to target Ukraine's energy infrastructure, leaving more than one million people without electricity and water, the European Union remains firmly at Ukraine's side to help its people endure another winter under attack.

title: EU allocates over €123 million in humanitarian aid for Latin America and Caribbean in 2026
content: In a year of shrinking global aid, the EU is choosing solidarity, delivering life-saving aid and standing by international humanitarian law. Part of this aid will also bolster disaster preparedness and response, given the region's high exposure to natural hazards.

title: EU allocates €63 million in response to the crisis in Myanmar and its impact on neighbouring countries
content: The European Commission has allocated €63 million in humanitarian aid to help populations affected by the ongoing conflict in Myanmar, as well as to support Rohingya refugees living in neighbouring countries, in particular in Bangladesh.
